title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
summary: This talk presents a layered approach to maintaining brand voice in AI systems, breaking voice into four components: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach draws on production experience from wedding venue, personal companion, and missing persons applications to explain why single-pass prompt instructions fail at scale.
writeup: Isadora Martin-Dye argues that single-pass tone instructions fail at scale and proposes layering brand voice into four components: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and a deterministic post-generation veto. The framework separates what never changes from what shifts by context, anchors style with examples rather than abstract directives, and adds a rule-based filter after generation to catch violations. The takeaways draw on production work across wedding venue, personal companion, and missing persons applications, where one-shot prompts proved insufficient for consistent voice.
